topic.what: SIGMA-RS: a modular Rust software stack for keyed-verification anonymous credential protocols
topic.how: layered architecture with CMZ, SIGMA-COMPILER, SIGMA-PROOFS, and SPONGEFISH crates providing declarative macros, proof composition, and Fiat-Shamir transformations
topic.why_matters: reduces duplicated effort, minimizes attack surface, enables cryptographic agility, and simplifies deployment of privacy-preserving credential systems

characteristics.what_it_measures: lines of code to implement credential protocols
characteristics.context: SIGMA-RS vs zkp library across 10 Lox protocols

characteristics.what_itasures: time to create credential proofs on client side
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 (Lox) and Table 3 (OONI) measurements on AMD Ryzen 9 7940HS
characteristics.context: Native runtime, 10 runs averaged, Framework 16 laptop

characteristics.what_it_measures: time to verify credential proofs on server side
client proof generation time in WebAssembly
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 wasm vs native client timings

novelty: layered modular stack combining credential specification, proof compilation, and Fiat-Shamir transformation
automatic disjunction invariant enforcement via statement tree rewriting
constant-time OR and threshold proofs using oblivious compaction
SPONGEFISH supporting XOF, permutation, and compression-function-based Fiat-Shamir
generates compact Rust code at compile-time avoiding runtime overhead
open_problems: soundness failures from incorrect encoding in Fiat-Shamir transcripts
optimizing oblivious compaction for better threshold proof performance
improving macro expressiveness and error messages
additional Fiat-Shamir transformations and correlation-intractable hash functions
